title: AI Rigor Detection - framework that detects performative rigor in AI-generated analysis; publication status is separated from validation status

fields.vision: A rigor-detection framework for AI outputs that look scholarly, structured, or cited while failing to do the underlying evidentiary work.
fields.mechanism: It separates the appearance of rigor from actual warrant: citation binding, source relevance, reasoning continuity, rubric fit, counterclaim handling, claim strength, Goodhart risk, adversarial-gaming flags, and validation limits are inspected as distinct failure surfaces.
fields.built_components: The specification is in place and the validation infrastructure reports measured performance at F1 0.84 under its current labeled test-set conditions. Publication status is not used as proof of the validation claim. Open questions, surfaced by the framework itself: the human calibration pilot is planned but hasn't been executed yet, so the F1 numbers rest on a single coder's labels rather than multi-rater consensus. The Goodhart blocking gap is unresolved — the framework flags Goodhart-style adversarial gaming but doesn't yet block it. And RSC-001 honestly named its own zero recall and meets its own retirement clause; the framework's self-discipline is what surfaced the failure.
fields.source_utilization: Source use is organized by role: method authorities, domain evidence, technical background, implementation evidence, and claim limits. The page should show why each source matters; direct quotations and stronger factual claims still require exact passage anchors before publication.
fields.plan: From specification-with-measured-validation to calibrated framework with multi-rater authority. Four discrete next studies move the framework forward; each is a contained piece of work, not a re-architecture. Step 01 Multi-rater pilot Lock authoritative threshold cutoffs against consensus ground truth; retire single-coder F1 dependency. Step 02 LLM-as-judge module Close the Goodhart gap — convert adversarial-gaming flags into blocks rather than alerts. Step 03 RSC-001 retirement The single-coder rule retires per its own clause once the multi-rater authority lands. Step 04 Manifest reconciliation Either build the named-but-missing files, or trim the governance manifest to what exists.
fields.status: Framework specified and validation claim kept as measured in the page; journal-publication status is not asserted without an official record.
fields.remaining_gaps: Confirm any journal submission or publication record before using published-article language; close multi-rater calibration and Goodhart-blocking gaps before strengthening the validation claim.
fields.detailed_source_vision: A framework for telling whether an AI's analytical output is substantively rigorous or just performing rigor — formatted citations that don't bind to source, hedged language that's decorative not honest, structured arguments that mimic academic form without doing the underlying work. RFGP names the failure modes, then tests whether the framework can detect them under defined validation conditions.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: Point the framework at an AI-generated output. Each of twelve contracts hunts for a specific failure mode of fake rigor.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: A rigor-detection framework for AI outputs that look scholarly, structured, or cited while failing to do the underlying evidentiary work.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: It separates the appearance of rigor from actual warrant: citation binding, source relevance, reasoning continuity, rubric fit, counterclaim handling, claim strength, Goodhart risk, adversarial-gaming flags, and validation limits are inspected as distinct failure surfaces.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It matters because AI-assisted analysis can fail by sounding careful; the project gives reviewers a vocabulary for detecting rigor theater and a path toward testing it against labeled rigorous and performative examples before it becomes accepted judgment.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: rendered correction table.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.

researcher_process_map.summary: A rigor-detection framework for AI outputs that look scholarly, structured, or cited while failing to do the underlying evidentiary work.
researcher_process_map.final_use_case: It matters because AI-assisted analysis can fail by sounding careful; the project gives reviewers a vocabulary for detecting rigor theater and a path toward testing it against labeled rigorous and performative examples before it becomes accepted judgment.
